impact: When CCP ships a new SDE (new systems, new gate connections like Yulai↔Manifest in the recent expansion), the admin "Force Update SDE" button only refreshes DB tables. The k-space star map at /map reads three static JSON files (systems.json, edges.json, regions.json) baked into the Vite bundle at build time, so the on-screen topology stays stale until someone manually runs `python scripts/build_map_data.py` and re-deploys the frontend. W-space map is unaffected (it reads from /api/map/wormholes-data/* which is DB-backed). Fix options: (a) add a FastAPI endpoint that serves these three JSONs from a writable runtime path, and have the SDE update flow regenerate them from the now-fresh DB tables; (b) call build_map_data.py from the SDE update flow and write into the runtime path; (c) leave static files for caching but also expose a runtime-override endpoint when fresher data exists on disk. Option (a) is cleanest — DB → JSON via the same projection logic build_map_data.py already does, written to /data/map/*.json, served by FastAPI with no-store. Manually unblocked on 2026-05-19 by running build_map_data.py locally and committing the regenerated JSONs (commit 7c8f43d).
title: SDE force-update doesn't refresh k-space star map static JSONs
